title: Recettear: An Item Shop's Tale

title: Recettear: An Item Shop's Tale
notes:
No audio.  The game does not run when the game's Screen Resolution is set to 1280x960 and Fullscreen.  Save file from Windows loads with no issue.
On average, loading times are a bit slower.  It is particularly slow when returning to the shop from the town map, departing to dungeons from the Adventurer's Guild, and loading the game.

gpu: Intel Open Source Technology Center Mesa DRI Intel(R) Ivybridge Mobile x86/MMX/SSE2
notes: No Audio :(
title: Recettear: An Item Shop's Tale

notes: Sound fixed by executing: WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/70400/pfx winetricks directmusic sound=alsa. See also: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/5#issuecomment-429343368
title: Recettear: An Item Shop's Tale

notes: Fixed audio by following https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/5#issuecomment-429343368
title: Recettear: An Item Shop's Tale

notes: No sound out of the box. Look up the tip in the linked Github issue regarding running `WINEPREFX=$STEAMAPPS/compatdata/70400/pfx/ winetricks directmusic sound=alsa` to get everything running perfectly.
title: Recettear: An Item Shop's Tale

notes: As far as I can tell everything but the audio works. Some have had luck by installing some other libraries through winetricks, but I haven't managed to get it working. Which is sad cause the music in this game is actually pretty good
title: Recettear: An Item Shop's Tale

notes: I've tried all the tricks mentioned underneath, but I just can't get the sound working with just that. The rest seemingly works fine, though.

title: Recettear: An Item Shop's Tale
notes:
No audio.  The game does not run when the game's Screen Resolution is set to 1280x960 and Fullscreen.  Save file from Windows loads with no issue.
On average, loading times are a bit slower.  It is particularly slow when returning to the shop from the town map, departing to dungeons from the Adventurer's Guild, and loading the game.
